steps.title: Start by dividing by N
steps.content: Begin with $$P = N(19 - C).$$ Divide both sides by $N$ to undo the multiplication: $$\frac{P}{N} = 19 - C.$$

steps.title: Solve for C
steps.content: Rearrange to isolate $C$. Add $C$ to both sides and subtract $\frac{P}{N}$: $$C = 19 - \frac{P}{N}.$$

steps.title: Compare with each choice
steps.content:
• A. $C=\frac{19+P}{N}$ — would require $P=NC-19$, not the given form.
  • B. $C=\frac{19-P}{N}$ — would require dividing 19 by $N$, but 19 was never divided.
  • C. $C=19+\frac{P}{N}$ — wrong sign on the fraction.
  • D. $C=19-\frac{P}{N}$ — matches the derivation exactly.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
